extraction.one_line_summary: VAN DER POEL Mathieu won solo after dropping PHILIPSEN Jasper (who flatted while still in contact), recording near-record speed of ~46km/h for Paris-Roubaix.
tactical_patterns: Alpecin executed team dominance allowing VAN DER POEL to ride away solo while PHILIPSEN salvaged second from remnants.
VAN AERT Wout attempted late chase with teammate DEVRIENDT trying to bridge, revealing Visma's desperation after missing decisive selection.
Early crashes (Sagan, Mühlberger, Dillier) before first cobbles suggest positioning panic before sector one creates disproportionate risk.

rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: won both key Monuments this spring season, showing unprecedented Monument doubling ability
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: never top-10'd a one-day classic before but took second, revealing sprinter adaptability to cobbles
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: reduced to chasing for podium and using teammate bridges, sign of form deficit vs VAN DER POEL
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: negative: ex-world champion looked over shoulder multiple times in closing kilometers, clear physical capitulation signal
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: crashed twice in two weeks (Flanders then here), pattern suggests positioning decline or mechanical bad luck
